description:
You are a DevSecOps engineer who must express a “policy-as-code” rule via the
    user-level crontab.  

    Goal  
    ----  
    1. Create (or update) the current user’s crontab so that it **contains one,
       and only one, job line**:  

           15 */6 * * 1-5 /usr/local/bin/scan_secrets.sh --mode=ci-cd # DevSecOps policy

       • Meaning: run the secrets-scanning script every weekday (Mon–Fri) at the
         15-minute mark of every 6th hour (00:15, 06:15, 12:15, 18:15).  
       • No additional comments, blank lines, or legacy jobs may remain in the
         crontab when you are done. The job line itself must appear **exactly as
         shown** above—including the single space before the trailing comment
         and the final “DevSecOps policy” words.  
       • You must accomplish this with a single terminal command
         (pipelines/redirects are fine; do not open an editor).  

    2. Immediately afterward, **export** the entire resulting crontab to the
       following file so that automated controls can pick it up:  

           /home/user/devsecops/policy/cron_baseline.log

       • The directory path must be created if it does not yet exist.  
       • The file must contain exactly the output of `crontab -l` after your
         change, terminated by a single trailing newline.  
       • Do not add any other text to this file.  

    3. The task is complete when both conditions hold:  
       a) `crontab -l` shows only the single job line specified in step 1.  
       b) The file /home/user/devsecops/policy/cron_baseline.log exists and its
          sole line (plus trailing newline) is byte-for-byte identical to the
          crontab output.  

    Output format that the automated test will check  
    -----------------------------------------------  
    The grader will perform two checks:

    • File contents check  
      Command run by grader:  
          cat /home/user/devsecops/policy/cron_baseline.log  
      Expected output (including the newline at end):  
          15 */6 * * 1-5 /usr/local/bin/scan_secrets.sh --mode=ci-cd # DevSecOps policy\n

    • Crontab check  
      Command run by grader:  
          crontab -l  
      Expected output (identical to above, newline included).  

    You do NOT need to display anything in the assistant response; simply
    perform the required terminal actions so that the on-disk artefacts and the
    crontab are in the state described.
truth:
The file /home/user/devsecops/policy/cron_baseline.log must exist when the
    grader runs and its content must be exactly:

15 */6 * * 1-5 /usr/local/bin/scan_secrets.sh --mode=ci-cd # DevSecOps policy\n

    (That is: the literal characters shown, followed by a single Unix newline.)

    The current user’s crontab, obtained via `crontab -l`, must contain exactly
    the same single line and nothing else.

    No other files are required. Standard user permissions (0644 for the
    cron_baseline.log file and 0755 for each directory in the path) are
    acceptable.
